extra_descs.desc: It is a small simple cup.

long_desc: A cup has been set here.

extra_descs.desc: It is a small simple cup.

long_desc: A cup has been set here.

extra_descs.desc: It is a large simple cup.

long_desc: A cup has been set here.

extra_descs.desc: It is a large, clean bottle.  There is a large label pasted on the side.

You've been ripped off!  It says here at the bottom of the label, in really
really small letters, 'Bottled in the Midgaard branch of Evian Inc.  This water
is from the natural, mostly clean River of Midgaard.  '

long_desc: A bottle of Evian natural spring water is here.

extra_descs.desc: It is a fairly big canteen.  Looks like it can hold a lot of liquid.

long_desc: A canteen has been set on the ground here.

extra_descs.desc:
This vest is made out of thick cloth.  It has bright gold buttons running
down the middle of it, and it has two small pockets on either side.

long_desc: A red ushers vest has been left here.

extra_descs.desc: This is a small flashlight, used by the ushers at the theater.

long_desc: A small black metal flashlight is lying here.

extra_descs.desc:
This pair of gloves looks fairly new.  The palms have an extra strip of
leather across them, while the tops have a layer of thick yellow fabric.

long_desc: A pair of thick leather work gloves have been left here.
short_desc: a pair of leather work gloves

extra_descs.desc:
This pair of overalls looks quite big.  They're a dark blue color, with a
large pocket over the chest.

long_desc: A pair of denim overalls have been discarded here.

extra_descs.desc: This jacket looks pretty nice.  It seems to fit you perfectly.

long_desc: An expensive looking black suit jacket is sitting here.

extra_descs.desc:
The shirt has no patterns or colors on it whatsover.  It's a thin white
shirt with white buttons.

long_desc: A white dress shirt has been neatly folded here.

extra_descs.desc: This pair of dress pants feel soft, and seem to fit you well.

long_desc: A pair of black dress pants is lying here.
short_desc: a pair of black dress pants

extra_descs.desc:
These shoes look quite comfortable.  The leather is soft, and has an
elaborate pattern printed on it.

long_desc: A pair of men's black leather dress shoes are sitting here.
short_desc: a pair of men's leather dress shoes

extra_descs.desc: This dress is very nice.  It's rather tight, but it fits you quite well.

long_desc: A fancy black dress has been left here.

extra_descs.desc:
These shoes look quite comfortable.  The leather is soft, the heels aren't
too high.

long_desc: A pair of women's dress heels are sitting here.

extra_descs.desc: This bow tie looks brand new.  It would go well with a suit.

long_desc: A black bow tie is here, waiting for you to pick it up.

extra_descs.desc:
It is probably the biggest key you have seen in your life.  It is made from
polished gold and has various patterns on it along with the Midgaard Coat of
Arms.

long_desc: The very big City Key is lying here.

extra_descs.desc:
This PDA looks pretty complicated.  It's a small black box, about the size of
a small book.  On the screen are several icons.

long_desc: A PDA (Personal Digital Assistant) is lying here.

extra_descs.desc:
A cellphone is a pretty worthless item.  Why use a cellphone when you can use
a regular phone?  And besides, on a MUD, you can magically communicate without
phones anyway.

long_desc: A black cell phone is here.

extra_descs.desc:
This laptop looks brand new.  Black in color, it features a 1.  6 GHz
processor, 512 MB of RAM, a 64 MB video card, and a 40x DVD/CD burner.  Pretty
fancy.

long_desc: A brand new laptop is here, waiting to be stolen.
A huge sack of gold coins sits here.
short_desc: a huge sack of gold coins

long_desc: A long sword has been left here.

extra_descs.desc:
It isn't so much that the meat looks poisoned or anything, but that you just
are not sure of its origins.  You doubt that a hunter would drop a side of
venison or rabbit meat...  What in the world could this meat have come from, you
wonder...

long_desc: A rather dubious looking piece of meat is on the ground here.

extra_descs.desc:
x-------------------------------------------------x
   |                                            pg 1 |
   |           THE STREET VENDOR FROM HELL           |
   |                   by Crazyman                   |
   |                                                 |
   |  (The play begins on a street in downtown       |
   |  New Sparta. It's about noon, and it's          |
   |  raining. Joe Smith is seated on a bench,       |
   |  along with a cop.)                             |
   |                                                 |
   |  Joe: Oh, how I wish it would stop raining! I   |
   |       grow weary of this foul weather.          |
   |                                                 |
   |  Cop: Actually, I like this rain. It gives me   |
   |       an excuse to sit in the 7-11 and eat      |
   |       donuts.                                   |
   |                                                 |
   |  Joe: Yes, you would like that.                 |
   |                                                 |
   |   (A high pitched scream pierces the silence.)  |
   |                                                 |
   |  Joe: What the hell was that? Is somebody       |
   |       trying to kill Abe again?                 |
   |                                                 |
   |  Cop: I don't know. I'll go check it out.       |
   |       (The cop stands up, and hurries down      |
   |       the street.)                              |
   |                                                 |
   |  Joe: (To himself) I think I'm heading home.    |
   |       (He gets up to leave.)                    |
   |                                                 |
   x-------------------------------------------------x

long_desc: The first page of a play script is lying here.
short_desc: the first page of a script

extra_descs.desc:
x-------------------------------------------------x
   |                                            pg 2 |
   |                                                 |
   |  (A street vendor dashes out from behind a      |
   |  building, and rushes over to Joe)              |
   |                                                 |
   |  Vendor: Good afternoon good sir! May I inter-  |
   |          est you in this beautiful gold watch?  |
   |          (The vendor pulls out a gold watch     |
   |          from his pocket, obviously one of      |
   |          many.)                                 |
   |                                                 |
   |  Joe: Is it stolen? It doesn't matter anyway,   |
   |       I don't need another watch. Besides, I    |
   |       don't even any money right now.           |
   |                                                 |
   |  Vendor: (Snarling) Look son, I want to sell    |
   |          you this watch. It's not stolen, and   |
   |          it's only 50 coins, a very reasonable  |
   |          price.                                 |
   |                                                 |
   |  Joe: That's true, it is a reasonable price.    |
   |       My answer is still no.                    |
   |                                                 |
   |  Vendor: Ok, this is what I'm going to do. (He  |
   |          reaches inside his coat and pulls      |
   |          out a switchblade.) You're going to    |
   |          buy this watch, or I'll cut you        |
   |          bitch. I've got 5 kids to feed.        |
   |                                                 |
   x-------------------------------------------------x

long_desc: The second page of a play script is lying here.
short_desc: the second page of a script

extra_descs.desc:
x-------------------------------------------------x
   |                                            pg 3 |
   |                                                 |
   |  Joe: Put that shit away man. I told you, I     |
   |       don't have the money for that.            |
   |                                                 |
   |  Vendor: That's it. (He slashes Joe with his    |
   |          knife, cutting him in the arm.)        |
   |                                                 |
   |  Joe: Help! Police! I'm being murdered!         |
   |                                                 |
   |  Vendor: Shut the hell up. You're gonna be      |
   |          sorry you didn't buy this from me.     |
   |          (He puts the watch away, and pulls out |
   |          a handgun. He cocks the trigger, and   |
   |          points it at Joe's chest.)             |
   |                                                 |
   |  Joe: No!!!!                                    |
   |                                                 |
   |  Vendor: Good-bye, friend. (He pulls the        |
   |          trigger, and the bullet rips through)  |
   |          Joe's body.)                           |
   |                                                 |
   |  Joe: (Falling to the ground.) You bastard.     |
   |       How unfair life can be! (He dies.)        |
   |                                                 |
   |  Vendor: Haha! Hopefully the next customer      |
   |          will be more cooperative!              |
   |                                                 |
   |                  T H E   E N D                  |
   |                                                 |
   x-------------------------------------------------x

long_desc: The third page of a script is lying here.
short_desc: the third page of a script

extra_descs.desc:
This looks like a very useful mop.  It has a soft cloth end, which looks like
it would be great at washing a floor.  The wooden handle is quite thick and
hard.  It would make an idea weapon.

long_desc: A wooden handled mop has been left here.

extra_descs.desc:
This is a curling broom.  It has a long thin plastic handle, and an end
consisting of rough fabric stretched over a piece of foam attached to a wooden
block.  Curlers use these to sweep the ice in front of the rocks.

long_desc: A plastic curling broom has been left.

extra_descs.desc:
The curling rock is made of a gray stone, and has a red handle and red
stripes on it.  It feels quite heavy.

long_desc: A red curling rock has been left here.

extra_descs.desc:
The curling rock is made of a gray stone, and has a yellow handle and yellow
stripes on it.  It feels quite heavy.

long_desc: A yellow curling rock has been left here.
A cup of coffee has been set here.

extra_descs.desc:
This hamburger was made at the Newbie Zoo.  It's still in its foil wrapper,
so it's still warm.

long_desc: A tasty looking hamburger has been left here.

extra_descs.desc:
This box of poutine is quite large.  For those who don't know, poutine is
composed of french fries covered with gravy and cheese curds.

long_desc: A box of cheesy poutine has been left here.

extra_descs.desc: This hot dog was made at the Newbie Zoo.  It looks pretty tasty.

long_desc: A foot-long is lying here.
A bottle of water has been left here.
A bag of potato chips has been left here.
A box of pretzels is here.
A sturdy key is lying here.
A shiny silver key has been left here.
